question.title: Recommend a wine
question.prompt: Respond to the customer as the sommelier.
question.instructions: Record one answer for each of your turns.
question.learning_objective: Recommend a wine and justify the choice in a realistic customer conversation.

content.scenario: A customer needs a wine for a dinner with grilled fish.

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: Good evening. I'm looking for a wine for a dinner party.

turns.speaker: sommelier
turns.prompt: Open the interaction and ask what food will be served.
turns.objective: Greet the customer and ask about the food.
turns.hint: Think about a natural opening question.
turns.retry_hint: Try using: What will you be serving?

recommended_language: Good evening. How can I help?

turns.speaker: customer
turns.text: We're having grilled fish, but one guest doesn't like very dry wines.

turns.speaker: sommelier
turns.prompt: Recommend one wine and explain the pairing.
turns.direction: Give a clear recommendation without sounding overly technical.
turns.context: The guest prefers a wine that is not extremely dry.
turns.objective: Recommend a suitable wine and justify the pairing.
turns.hint: Mention the style or origin of the wine.
turns.retry_hint: Make the connection between the fish and your recommendation explicit.

rubric.label: Interaction
rubric.description: Responds naturally to the customer's information.

rubric.label: Professional language
rubric.description: Uses suitable recommendation and pairing language.
